lesson.title: JSON
lesson.summary: Marshal and unmarshal JSON — Go's built-in encoding/json package.

sections.heading: Marshalling — Go to JSON
sections.bodyHtml:
Use json.Marshal to convert a Go value to JSON bytes. Export the fields you want (capital letters), use struct tags to control names:

type User struct {
    ID    int    `json:"id"`
    Name  string `json:"name"`
    Email string `json:"email,omitempty"`
}

u := User{ID: 1, Name: "Alice"}
b, _ := json.Marshal(u)
fmt.Println(string(b))  // {"id":1,"name":"Alice"}

Tags control the JSON field name (json:"name"), omit empty fields (omitempty), or skip a field entirely (json:"-").

sections.heading: Unmarshalling — JSON to Go
sections.bodyHtml:
Use json.Unmarshal to parse JSON into a Go struct. Unknown fields are ignored by default:

data := []byte(`{"id":2,"name":"Bob"}`)
var u User
json.Unmarshal(data, &u)
fmt.Println(u.Name)  // Bob

For unstructured data, use map[string]any:

var m map[string]any
json.Unmarshal(data, &m)
fmt.Println(m["name"])  // Bob

sections.heading: Working with streams
sections.bodyHtml:
For JSON streams or large payloads, use json.NewDecoder and json.NewEncoder:

// Decode from a file
f, _ := os.Open("users.json")
defer f.Close()
var users []User
json.NewDecoder(f).Decode(&users)

// Encode to stdout
json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout).Encode(users)

The encoder automatically indents nothing by default. Use json.MarshalIndent for pretty output.

sections.heading: Your turn
sections.bodyHtml:
Open practice/json-io/main.go.

Define a Product struct with ID, Name, Price fields. Create a slice with two products, marshal to JSON, and print the result.

questions.prompt: How do you control the JSON field name for a struct field?

options.label: Name the Go field with the JSON name

questions.prompt: What does json:"-" do in a struct tag?

options.label: Omits the field when it is empty
Skips the field entirely (never included in JSON)
Uses "-" as the JSON field name

exercises.title: Product JSON
exercises.instructions: Define Product struct, marshal a slice of 2 products to JSON, and print.
